description:
You are a backup-integrity engineer.  
    A daily backup job has produced the log file  
    /home/user/backup/logs/backup_2023-09-15.log  

    Goal: isolate every line that shows data-integrity problems and save them in a new report.

    1. A line represents a problem when it contains either of the two literal tokens  
       “FILE_MISSING” or “CHECKSUM_MISMATCH” (case-sensitive, no extra spaces).  
       • These tokens appear only inside lines already tagged with either “[ERROR]” or “[WARNING]”.  
       • Ignore every other line.

    2. Create the directory /home/user/backup/reports if it does not yet exist.

    3. Write all matching lines, in the original order and without any alteration, to the new file  
       /home/user/backup/reports/2023-09-15_integrity_summary.log  
       • Preserve exact spacing and timestamps.  
       • Each line must end with a single UNIX newline character “\n”.  
       • Do not add a header, footer, blank lines, or any other text.

    4. After the file is written, print exactly one line to STDOUT of the form  
       Total problematic files: N  
       where N is the integer count of lines you just copied.  
       • Print nothing else to STDOUT or STDERR after that line.  
       • The line must end with a newline.

    Hints (not commands): you will probably need 2–3 shell commands such as `grep` with a basic or extended regex and `wc -l`.

    The automated tests will verify BOTH:
       a. The precise contents of /home/user/backup/reports/2023-09-15_integrity_summary.log  
       b. That your final console output is exactly the single line described above.
truth:

    ------------------------

    /home/user/backup/
    ├── logs/
    │   └── backup_2023-09-15.log
    └── reports/         (exists but may be empty)

    Exact contents of /home/user/backup/logs/backup_2023-09-15.log
    (each line followed by '\n'):

    1  2023-09-15 02:10:34 [INFO] Backup started
    2  2023-09-15 02:12:11 [OK] file: /var/data/db1.sql (checksum=ae34bc...)
    3  2023-09-15 02:12:15 [ERROR] FILE_MISSING /var/data/config.yml
    4  2023-09-15 02:12:16 [OK] file: /var/data/report.pdf (checksum=5b9a7d...)
    5  2023-09-15 02:12:20 [WARNING] CHECKSUM_MISMATCH /var/data/image.png
    6  2023-09-15 02:12:25 [OK] file: /var/data/archive.tar.gz (checksum=9d4e8f...)
    7  2023-09-15 02:12:35 [ERROR] FILE_MISSING /var/data/old_backup.tar.gz
    8  2023-09-15 02:13:10 [INFO] Backup finished: duration=2m36s

    Expected artefacts after successful completion
    ----------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/backup/reports/2023-09-15_integrity_summary.log must exist with exactly:

       2023-09-15 02:12:15 [ERROR] FILE_MISSING /var/data/config.yml\n
       2023-09-15 02:12:20 [WARNING] CHECKSUM_MISMATCH /var/data/image.png\n
       2023-09-15 02:12:35 [ERROR] FILE_MISSING /var/data/old_backup.tar.gz\n

    2. The agent’s final STDOUT line (after all commands) must be exactly:

       Total problematic files: 3\n
    -->
